rubric_en: Stop lowering the age of criminal responsibility
rubric_zh: 反对降低刑事责任年龄

paragraphs.en: HOW DO YOU judge whether children are responsible for their actions? The answer often depends on age. Given how slowly human character develops, most countries do not hold the youngest to be criminally responsible, even if they commit heinous acts. In the eyes of the law the least mature, who cannot grasp the consequences of what they do, are reckoned to be doli incapax—incapable of evil.
paragraphs.zh: 你如何判断孩子是否应对自己的行为负责？答案往往取决于年龄。由于人的品格发展很慢，大多数国家都不会将最幼小者追究刑事责任，即使他们犯下了极其残忍的罪行。在法律上，最不成熟、无法理解自己行为后果的人被认定为“doli incapax”——即尚无作恶能力。

paragraphs.en: Yet that raises a second question: how old is old enough to count? Countries answer this in wildly different ways, as they wrestle with the task of defining the age of criminal responsibility. Now, regrettably, lawmakers in many places are pushing it downwards. In February Argentina lowered its age from 16 to 14. Others, from the Maldives to South Korea, may do something similar. Gangs have been a scourge in Sweden for many years. It now plans to reduce the age of responsibility for the most serious crimes. Last month Northern Ireland blocked a motion that would have raised the age from just ten—the joint-lowest in Europe—to 14.
paragraphs.zh: 然而这引出了第二个问题：多大才算够大？各国在此问题上的答案大相径庭，因为它们都在艰难界定刑事责任年龄。可惜的是，许多地方的立法者正朝着降低年龄的方向推进。阿根廷在二月将年龄从16岁降至14岁。其他国家，从马尔代夫到韩国，也可能做出类似调整。多年来，帮派在瑞典一直是一个严重祸害。瑞典现在计划下调对最严重犯罪的责任年龄。上个月，北爱尔兰否决了一项将年龄从仅10岁（欧洲共同最低年龄）提高到14岁的提案。

paragraphs.en: Politicians are listening to voters angry about dramatic and well-publicised crimes—and you can understand why. Indonesians were horrified when a 12-year-old stabbed her mother 26 times, killing her. In Colombia a 15-year-old boy last year shot and killed a senator campaigning to be president. In Sweden and elsewhere, gangs use children to attack properties or people.
paragraphs.zh: 政客们在回应选民对那些戏剧性且广受媒体关注的罪行所引发的愤怒，而这点可以理解。印尼人震惊于一名12岁女孩刺伤自己母亲26次并将其杀害。在哥伦比亚，去年一名15岁男孩枪杀了正在竞选总统的一名参议员。在瑞典及其他地区，帮派动用儿童去袭击财产或人。

paragraphs.en: Criminals like to recruit young foot soldiers because they are cheap and pliable. They often lack the impulse-control and judgment that most adults possess. Neuroscientists suggest that the brain keeps developing well into adulthood, perhaps even into the mid-20s, which may be why so much crime is committed by the young. Teens are often enticed into gangs by the promise of quick money. Criminals also calculate that the youngest may dodge punishment if caught. In Britain drug-dealers use children precisely because they are under-age.
paragraphs.zh: 犯罪分子喜欢招募年幼的“步兵”，因为他们成本低且容易被操控。这些孩子往往缺乏大多数成年人具备的冲动控制和判断力。神经科学家认为，大脑的发展持续到成年以后，甚至可能延续到二十多岁，这也许就是为何那么多犯罪由年轻人实施的原因。青少年常被帮派许以快钱诱惑而卷入其中。犯罪分子也会计算，认为最年轻的成员即使被抓，也可能逃过惩罚。在英国，毒贩之所以专门用孩子当马仔，正是因为他们未成年。

paragraphs.en: You can see, therefore, why lawmakers want to criminalise the acts of ever younger people. Authorities may hope that doing so will remove an incentive for gangs to recruit them. They should reconsider, if only because lowering the age of responsibility is unjust—especially if that involves children younger than the low teens.
paragraphs.zh: 因此你可以理解，为什么立法者想要把更年幼者的行为定为犯罪。当局可能希望通过此举消除帮派招募他们的动机。若只是因为降低责任年龄本身就是不公正的——尤其是当这意味着让十几岁以下的儿童也被纳入其中——他们就应当重新考虑。

paragraphs.en: Even if you disagree with the moral argument, you should heed the practical one. Experience suggests that lowering the age will not work. When Denmark took it from 15 to 14 in 2010, crime rates rose. The country reversed course soon after. Britain found that, when the age of responsibility drops, gangs just recruit even younger children.
paragraphs.zh: 即使你不同意道德层面的观点，也应当重视现实层面的论点。经验表明，降低责任年龄并不会奏效。2010年丹麦将年龄从15岁降至14岁后，犯罪率上升，该国很快便改变了方向。英国发现，当责任年龄下降时，帮派只是改为招募更年幼的孩子。

paragraphs.en: Prosecuting young children as criminals is also a way of ensuring that ever more of them will emerge from the system as hardened villains. In Queensland, Australia, where ten-year-olds are counted as criminally responsible, 96% of children who are released from prison go on to reoffend within a year.
paragraphs.zh: 将年幼儿童当作罪犯起诉，也是在确保越来越多孩子最终从体系中走出来后，变成更冷酷的恶棍。在澳大利亚昆士兰，十岁儿童就被视为要承担刑事责任，其中96%的儿童出狱后一年内再次犯罪。

paragraphs.en: It is wiser to try rehabilitation. That will often mean securing young wrongdoers safely away from wider society. But rather than punishing them, the goal should be to tackle the social, educational and other factors that first drew them into criminality. One focus should be on those brains. In Britain a fifth of children in the youth-justice system have learning difficulties. Another should be to deal with social problems early by, say, providing mentors. In Sweden around half of all kids who are investigated for a serious crime were flagged to social services for worrying behaviour before the age of 12.
paragraphs.zh: 更明智的做法是尝试康复。这通常意味着把年轻违法者安全地暂时与更广泛社会隔离开来。但与其惩罚他们，目标应当是处理促使他们初次走向犯罪的社会、教育等因素。一个重点应放在这些孩子的认知问题上。在英国，少年司法系统中每五个孩子里就有一个有学习困难。另一个重点应是尽早处理社会问题，例如安排导师。在瑞典，约有一半被调查涉嫌严重犯罪的孩子，在12岁以前就因为令人担忧的行为而被社工部门标记转介。

paragraphs.en: Nothing will stop all wrongdoing by children. There should be severe criminal penalties for adults who recruit and exploit the young. But the goal for children should be to create better childhoods, with fewer reasons to go into crime, and to prevent criminal children from becoming criminal adults. ■
paragraphs.zh: 没有办法让所有儿童不犯错。对于招募并剥削未成年人的成年人，应当施以严厉的刑事处罚。真正对儿童而言的目标，应是营造更健康的童年，减少他们走上犯罪道路的诱因，并防止有罪的未成年人在成长为成年人后继续犯罪。■

paragraphs.en: Subscribers to The Economist can sign up to our Opinion newsletter, which brings together the best of our leaders, columns, guest essays and reader correspondence.
paragraphs.zh: 《经济学人》订阅者可以注册我们的“观点”通讯，汇集我们最优秀的社论、专栏、客座文稿与读者来信。
